Hepatica is a perennial plant native to the mountainous regions of Europe, Asia, and North America. It is often referred to as liverwort due to its historical use in treating liver and gallbladder disorders. The health benefits of Hepatica include its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties, which make it useful in treating various ailments such as fever, digestive issues, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Hepatica include flavonoids, saponins, and tannins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
